title: No Title
caption: Supplementary Materials
context description:
We limited all our analysis to early-stage idiopathic Parkinson’s disease (PD) patients (i.e., diagnosed <13 months and no known predisposing genetic variation) to age and sex-matched controls, using PPMI as a discovery cohort and ICICLE-PD as a replication cohort (Fig. 1 [rid=Fig1] and Supplementary Table 1 [this file] ). 
Whilst we observed differential expression of specific BSJs, perhaps the more striking observation was an imbalance of BSJ fold changes, i.e., where the expression of the majority of BSJs tended to be reduced in PD compared to controls (PPMI imbalance = 0.09, Bonferroni -value = 9.2 × 10 , ICICLE-PD imbalance = 0.29, Bonferroni -value = 9.4 × 10 , Exact binomial test) (Fig. 4a [rid=Fig4] , Supplementary Data 10 [this file2] ). To explore whether reduced expression was specific to BSJs, we also examined the expression of other RNA types (Supplementary Fig. 7 [this file] , Supplementary Data 10 [this file2] ). Crucially, a reduction was not evident when examining the fold changes of FSJs (PPMI imbalance = 0.54, Bonferroni -value = 1.0, ICICLE-PD imbalance = 0.50, Bonferroni -value = 1.0, Exact binomial test) (Fig. 4a [rid=Fig4] , Supplementary Data 10 [this file2] ). Global gene expression also showed no reduction in PPMI (imbalance = 0.49, Bonferroni -value = 1.0), yet was significantly increased in ICICLE-PD (imbalance = 0.56, Bonferroni -value = 3.6 × 10 ) (Fig. 4a [rid=Fig4] , Supplementary Data 10 [this file2] ). 
This also provided evidence of a significant reduction in circRNA expression in PD (PPMI -value = 4.4 × 10 , ICICLE-PD -value = 8.6 × 10 , Wilcoxon rank-sum test) (Fig. 4b [rid=Fig4] ), yet no reduction in the expression of flanking FSJs, expression of circRNA host genes or total gene expression (Supplementary Fig. 8 [this file] ). 
All other classifiers had an AUC < 0.6 in the PPMI cohort (Fig. 5b [rid=Fig5] , Supplementary Table 2 [this file] ). All other unmentioned models achieved an AUC < 0.6 in the ICICLE-PD cohort (Fig. 5b [rid=Fig5] , Supplementary Table 2 [this file] ). 
Median sequencing depth was estimated as 89.2 (IQR = 15.7) million paired-end reads (Supplementary Fig. 1 [this file] ). 
Median sequencing depth was estimated as 107 (IQR = 31.2) million paired-end reads (Supplementary Figure 1 [this file] ). 
In addition, BSJs were only retained for downstream analysis if they were detected by at least two tools (Supplementary Figure 2 [this file] ). 
Principal component analysis was carried out and the first two principal components and was used to detect mismatches by visual inspection (Supplementary Fig. 3 [this file] ). After QC, the final PPMI dataset comprised of 259 PD and 161 controls; the ICICLE-PD dataset comprised of 48 PD patients and 48 controls (summarised in Supplementary Table 1 [this file] ). We observed no significant differences between the age or sex profiles of cases and controls in either dataset (Supplementary Table 1 [this file] ). 
At the sample level, we used univariate linear regression to identify technical sequencing metrics (obtained from Picard and SAMtools) that explained a high proportion of the variance (R > 0.5) associated with the first 10 principal components of gene and circRNA expression (Supplementary Figs. 4 [this file] a, b and 5a, b [this file] ). At the gene level, based on recommendations , we excluded highly correlated factors (Spearman’s ρ > 0.9, Supplementary Figs. 4 [this file] c and 5c [this file] ), subsequently quantifying the contribution of this reduced set of covariates to gene and circRNA expression variation using variancePartition v1.28.3 (Supplementary Figure 6 [this file] ).
Source Resource Name: Early-stage idiopathic Parkinson’s disease is associated with reduced circular RNA expression

In PPMI, we identified 44 significantly differentially expressed genes (log FC < −0.1/ > 0.1, FDR < 0.05), with 28 upregulated and 16 downregulated in PD (Fig. 2a [rid=Fig2] and Supplementary Data 1 [this file] ). Of these 44, and were similarly upregulated in ICICLE-PD (log FC > 0.1, FDR < 0.05) (Fig. 2a, b [rid=Fig2] and Supplementary Data 1 [this file] ). 
Volcano plot of differential expression in PPMI and ICICLE-PD, highlighted (orange) are the 44 genes significantly differentially expressed in PPMI (FDR < 0.05 and log fold change >0.1/ < −0.1) and the corresponding results in ICICLE-PD (Supplementary Data 1 [this file] ). 
We also observed significantly increased expression of , which encodes PKR, in both cohorts (PPMI -value = 0.010; ICICLE-PD -value = 0.019) (Fig. 4f [rid=Fig4] , Supplementary Data 1 [this file] ). 
This is consistent with our results, where the reduction in circRNAs in PD we observed coincides with increased expression of and (Fig. 4e, f [rid=Fig4] , Supplementary Data 1 [this file] ), as well as the upregulation of genes related to antiviral activity and the innate immune response (Fig. 2c [rid=Fig2] , Supplementary Data 2 [rid=MOESM4] , 3 [rid=MOESM5] ).

title: No Title
caption: Supplmentary Data 2
context description:
NES = Normalised Enrichment Score (Supplementary Data 2 [this file] ). 
After classifying into GO terms, we identified 97 biological processes, 11 molecular functions and 29 cellular components that were significantly enriched in PPMI and subsequently replicated in ICICLE-PD (FDR < 0.05 with the same direction of enrichment) (Supplementary Data 2 [this file] ). In contrast, ontologies related to the innate immune response, including antiviral and interferon signalling processes, tended to be increased in PD (the top 10 ranked by PPMI -value in each ontology are shown in Fig. 2c [rid=Fig2] with all results present in Supplementary Data 2 [this file] ). 
This, combined with the downregulation of protein synthesis genes and the upregulation of genes involved in the innate immune response (Fig. 2c [rid=Fig2] , Supplementary Data 2 [this file] ), suggests that the antiviral integrated stress response may be active in early-stage idiopathic PD. 
This is consistent with our results, where the reduction in circRNAs in PD we observed coincides with increased expression of and (Fig. 4e, f [rid=Fig4] , Supplementary Data 1 [rid=MOESM3] ), as well as the upregulation of genes related to antiviral activity and the innate immune response (Fig. 2c [rid=Fig2] , Supplementary Data 2 [this file] , 3 [rid=MOESM5] ). Protein synthesis is reduced in PD patient cell lines , consistent with the reduced expression of genes related to protein synthesis and ribosomal function we observed in PD (Fig. 2c [rid=Fig2] , Supplementary Data 2 [this file] , 3 [rid=MOESM5] ).
Source Resource Name: Early-stage idiopathic Parkinson’s disease is associated with reduced circular RNA expression

We explored whether differential expression of previously reported circRNA regulators may be contributing to the reduced circRNA abundances in PD (regulators and evidence are given in Supplementary Data 11 [this file] ). In PPMI, and were significantly increased in PD, with also increased in ICICLE-PD and significant after multiple testing correction (Fig. 4e [rid=Fig4] , Supplementary Data 11 [this file] ). encodes Ribonuclease L, which is known to be involved in the degradation of circRNA, subsequently allowing the activation of PKR as part of the integrated stress response . 
CircRNA expression is influenced by trans-acting proteins, including Ribonuclease L (RNase L, encoded by ) and adenosine deaminase RNA specific (ADAR, encoded by ), an enzyme responsible for A-I RNA editing that can modulate the base-pairing of reverse complementary matches in the flanking introns of circRNAs, influencing their biogenesis . was significantly increased in PPMI PD cases and showed a similar trend in ICICLE-PD (Supplementary Data 11 [this file] ).
